failed_cases.description: Public names import from the single module, including MetadataIndex and resolve_metadata, and exception classes inherit from ValueError.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Version, InvalidVersion, SpecifierSet, InvalidSpecifier, Requirement, InvalidRequirement, Marker, InvalidMarker, UndefinedEnvironmentName, default_environment, is_requirement_satisfied, MetadataIndex, resolve_metadata
print(Version.__name__, SpecifierSet.__name__, Requirement.__name__, Marker.__name__, MetadataIndex.__name__, resolve_metadata.__name__)
print(issubclass(InvalidVersion, ValueError), issubclass(InvalidSpecifier, ValueError), issubclass(InvalidRequirement, ValueError), issubclass(InvalidMarker, ValueError), issubclass(UndefinedEnvironmentName, ValueError))

failed_cases.description: SpecifierSet supports compatible release bounds.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import SpecifierSet
s1 = SpecifierSet('~=1.4')
s2 = SpecifierSet('~=1.4.5')
print([s1.contains(v) for v in ['1.4', '1.9', '2.0']])
print([s2.contains(v) for v in ['1.4.5', '1.4.9', '1.5']])

failed_cases.description: SpecifierSet excludes pre-releases by default and includes them when requested.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import SpecifierSet
s = SpecifierSet('>=1.0')
print(s.contains('1.1a1'))
print(s.contains('1.1a1', prereleases=True))
print(s.contains('1.1.dev1', prereleases=False))

failed_cases.description: Invalid specifier text raises InvalidSpecifier.
Requirement parses direct URLs and marker text as distinct fields.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement
r = Requirement('Demo @ https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl ; python_version >= "3.10"')
print(r.name)
print(r.url)
print(str(r.specifier))
print(type(r.marker).__name__)
print(str(r))

failed_cases.description: resolve_metadata exposes the documented projection keys and a simple semantic schema without mutating caller metadata.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import resolve_metadata
candidates = [
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['dep>=1; extra == "pdf"']},
    {'name': 'dep', 'version': '1.0'},
]
before = repr(candidates)
res = resolve_metadata(['App[PDF]>=1'], candidates)
print(sorted(res))
print(sorted(res['selected'].items()))
print(sorted((e['parent'], e['name'], tuple(e['extras'])) for e in res['edges']))
print(sorted((k, tuple(v)) for k, v in res['requested_extras'].items()))
print(sorted((r['source'], r['parent'], r['name'], tuple(r['extras'])) for r in res['requirements']))
print(repr(candidates) == before)

failed_cases.description: MetadataIndex add_candidate copies caller metadata and resolves selected versions, dependency edges, reverse dependents, and requested extras from the stored fact table.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import MetadataIndex
idx = MetadataIndex()
app = {'name': 'App_Main', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['Core_Lib>=1,<3', 'Plugin[Fast]>=1; extra == "speed"']}
idx.add_candidate(app)
idx.add_candidate({'name': 'core-lib', 'version': '1.0'})
idx.add_candidate({'name': 'core-lib', 'version': '2.0'})
idx.add_candidate({'name': 'core-lib', 'version': '3.0'})
idx.add_candidate({'name': 'plugin', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['speed-lib>=1; extra == "fast"']})
idx.add_candidate({'name': 'speed-lib', 'version': '1.0'})
app['requires'].append('leak>=1')
res = idx.resolve(['App-Main[Speed]>=1'])
print(sorted(res['selected'].items()))
print(sorted((e['parent'], e['name'], tuple(e.get('extras') or ())) for e in res['edges']))
print(sorted((k, tuple(v)) for k, v in res['requested_extras'].items()))
print(res['dependents'].get('leak'), res['revision'] >= 6)

failed_cases.description: A batch update and remove invalidates stale selection, exclusion, index, and reverse-dependent projections together.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import MetadataIndex
idx = MetadataIndex([
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['lib<4']},
    {'name': 'lib', 'version': '1.0'},
    {'name': 'lib', 'version': '2.0'},
    {'name': 'lib', 'version': '3.0'},
])
first = idx.resolve(['app>=1'])
idx.apply([
    {'action': 'update', 'candidate': {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['lib<3']}},
    {'action': 'remove', 'name': 'lib', 'version': '1.0'},
])
second = idx.resolve(['app>=1'])
print(sorted(first['selected'].items()), first['excluded']['lib'])
print(sorted(second['selected'].items()), second['excluded']['lib'])
print(idx.index()['lib'])
print(second['dependents']['lib'])

failed_cases.description: dependents_of is a reverse projection of the same active graph used by resolve, including transitive parent lookup.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import MetadataIndex
idx = MetadataIndex([
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['core>=1']},
    {'name': 'core', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['shared>=1']},
    {'name': 'shared', 'version': '1.0'},
    {'name': 'plugin', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['shared>=1']},
])
res = idx.resolve(['app>=1'])
print(sorted((k, tuple(v)) for k, v in res['dependents'].items()))
print(idx.dependents_of('shared', roots=['app>=1']))
print(idx.dependents_of('shared', roots=['app>=1'], transitive=True))
print(idx.dependents_of('Shared', roots=['plugin>=1']))

failed_cases.description: resolve_lock and apply_lock replay the same selected graph after unrelated candidate additions while fresh resolve can select a newer version.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import MetadataIndex
idx = MetadataIndex([
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['lib>=1']},
    {'name': 'lib', 'version': '1.0'},
    {'name': 'lib', 'version': '2.0'},
])
lock = idx.resolve_lock(['app>=1'])
idx.add_candidate({'name': 'lib', 'version': '3.0'})
idx.add_candidate({'name': 'other', 'version': '9.0'})
fresh = idx.resolve(['app>=1'])
replayed = idx.apply_lock(lock)
print(sorted(lock['selected'].items()))
print(sorted(fresh['selected'].items()))
print(sorted(replayed['selected'].items()))
print(replayed['lock_revision'] == lock['revision'], replayed['revision'] == idx.resolve(['app>=1'])['revision'])

from minipackaging import MetadataIndex
idx = MetadataIndex([
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['lib>=1']},
    {'name': 'lib', 'version': '1.0'},
])
lock = idx.resolve_lock(['app>=1'])
idx.apply([{'action': 'update', 'candidate': {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['missing>=1']}}])
before = idx.export_state()
try:
    idx.apply_lock(lock)
    print('accepted')
except ValueError:

print(idx.export_state() == before)
print(idx.index()['app'])

failed_cases.description: export_state/import_state replay the candidate table, resolver projections, reverse dependents, and lock behavior without relying on object identity.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import MetadataIndex
idx = MetadataIndex([
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['core>=1', 'docs[html]>=1; extra == "docs"']},
    {'name': 'core', 'version': '1.0'},
    {'name': 'docs', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['renderer>=1; extra == "html"']},
    {'name': 'renderer', 'version': '1.0'},
])
lock = idx.resolve_lock(['App[Docs]>=1'])
clone = MetadataIndex.import_state(idx.export_state())
a = idx.resolve(['App[Docs]>=1'])
b = clone.resolve(['App[Docs]>=1'])
replay = clone.apply_lock(lock)
print(clone.index() == idx.index())
print(a['selected'] == b['selected'], a['dependents'] == b['dependents'], a['requested_extras'] == b['requested_extras'])
print(sorted(replay['selected'].items()))
print(clone.dependents_of('renderer', roots=['app[docs]>=1'], transitive=True))

failed_cases.description: A failed mixed apply batch leaves export, revision, and resolver projections unchanged; a later valid batch updates all views together.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import MetadataIndex
idx = MetadataIndex([
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['lib>=1']},
    {'name': 'lib', 'version': '1.0'},
])
before_state = idx.export_state()
before_res = idx.resolve(['app>=1'])
try:
    idx.apply([
        {'action': 'add', 'candidate': {'name': 'tool', 'version': '1.0'}},
        {'action': 'update', 'candidate': {'name': 'missing', 'version': '1.0'}},
    ])
    print('accepted')
except ValueError:
    print('batch-failed')
print(idx.export_state() == before_state, idx.resolve(['app>=1'])['selected'] == before_res['selected'])
idx.apply([
    {'action': 'add', 'candidate': {'name': 'tool', 'version': '1.0'}},
    {'action': 'update', 'candidate': {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['lib>=1', 'tool>=1']}},
])
print(sorted(idx.resolve(['app>=1'])['selected'].items()))
print(idx.dependents_of('tool', roots=['app>=1']))

])

failed_cases.description: The stateless resolve_metadata convenience API and MetadataIndex.resolve expose equivalent semantic projections from the same candidate facts, while the index adds only lifecycle metadata.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import MetadataIndex, resolve_metadata
candidates = [
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['addon[fast]>=1; extra == "speed"']},
    {'name': 'addon', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['fast-lib>=1; extra == "fast"']},
    {'name': 'fast-lib', 'version': '1.0'},
]
roots = ['App[Speed]>=1']
stateless = resolve_metadata(roots, candidates)
idx = MetadataIndex(reversed(candidates))
stateful = idx.resolve(roots)
keys = ['selected', 'excluded', 'dependents', 'requested_extras']
print(all(stateless[k] == stateful[k] for k in keys))
print(sorted((e['parent'], e['name'], tuple(e.get('extras') or ())) for e in stateless['edges']) == sorted((e['parent'], e['name'], tuple(e.get('extras') or ())) for e in stateful['edges']))
print('revision' in stateful and 'index' in stateful and 'revision' not in stateless)
print(sorted(stateful['selected'].items()))

all_cases.description: Invalid version text raises InvalidVersion.
SpecifierSet applies inclusive and exclusive range clauses.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import SpecifierSet
s = SpecifierSet('>=1.2, <2.0')
print(str(s))
print([s.contains(v) for v in ['1.1', '1.2', '1.9', '2.0']])

all_cases.description: SpecifierSet supports equality, inequality, and wildcard release prefixes.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import SpecifierSet
print([SpecifierSet('==1.2.*').contains(v) for v in ['1.2', '1.2.5', '1.3']])
print([SpecifierSet('!=1.2.*').contains(v) for v in ['1.1', '1.2.9', '1.3']])

all_cases.description: SpecifierSet supports compatible release bounds.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import SpecifierSet
s1 = SpecifierSet('~=1.4')
s2 = SpecifierSet('~=1.4.5')
print([s1.contains(v) for v in ['1.4', '1.9', '2.0']])
print([s2.contains(v) for v in ['1.4.5', '1.4.9', '1.5']])

all_cases.description: SpecifierSet excludes pre-releases by default and includes them when requested.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import SpecifierSet
s = SpecifierSet('>=1.0')
print(s.contains('1.1a1'))
print(s.contains('1.1a1', prereleases=True))
print(s.contains('1.1.dev1', prereleases=False))

all_cases.description: Invalid specifier text raises InvalidSpecifier.
Requirement parses normalized names, sorted normalized extras, and canonical specifiers.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement
r = Requirement('My_Pkg[PDF,security-extra] >=1.0, <2')
print(r.name)
print(sorted(r.extras))
print(str(r.specifier))
print(str(r))

all_cases.description: Requirement parses direct URLs and marker text as distinct fields.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement
r = Requirement('Demo @ https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl ; python_version >= "3.10"')
print(r.name)
print(r.url)
print(str(r.specifier))
print(type(r.marker).__name__)
print(str(r))

all_cases.description: Requirement equality and hashing use normalized names and sorted extras.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement
a = Requirement('Pkg_Name[B,A]>=1')
b = Requirement('pkg-name[a,b] >= 1.0.0')
print(a == b)
print(len({a, b}))
print(str(b))

all_cases.description: Invalid requirement strings raise InvalidRequirement.
Marker evaluates equality and version comparisons against a caller environment.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Marker
env = {'python_version': '3.11', 'os_name': 'posix'}
print(Marker('python_version >= "3.8"').evaluate(env))
print(Marker('os_name == "nt"').evaluate(env))

all_cases.description: Marker honors boolean precedence and parentheses.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Marker
env = {'python_version': '3.9', 'sys_platform': 'linux', 'os_name': 'posix'}
print(Marker('python_version >= "3.8" and sys_platform == "linux"').evaluate(env))
print(Marker('python_version < "3.8" and (sys_platform == "linux" or os_name == "posix")').evaluate(env))

all_cases.description: Marker evaluates membership operators and requested extras.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Marker
print(Marker('sys_platform in "linux darwin"').evaluate({'sys_platform': 'linux'}))
print(Marker('extra == "security-extra"').evaluate({}, requested_extras={'Security_Extra'}))
print(Marker('extra == "docs"').evaluate({}, requested_extras={'Security_Extra'}))

all_cases.description: Marker syntax errors and missing environment variables use the documented exceptions.
resolve_metadata exposes the documented projection keys and a simple semantic schema without mutating caller metadata.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import resolve_metadata
candidates = [
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['dep>=1; extra == "pdf"']},
    {'name': 'dep', 'version': '1.0'},
]
before = repr(candidates)
res = resolve_metadata(['App[PDF]>=1'], candidates)
print(sorted(res))
print(sorted(res['selected'].items()))
print(sorted((e['parent'], e['name'], tuple(e['extras'])) for e in res['edges']))
print(sorted((k, tuple(v)) for k, v in res['requested_extras'].items()))
print(sorted((r['source'], r['parent'], r['name'], tuple(r['extras'])) for r in res['requirements']))
print(repr(candidates) == before)

all_cases.description: MetadataIndex add_candidate copies caller metadata and resolves selected versions, dependency edges, reverse dependents, and requested extras from the stored fact table.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import MetadataIndex
idx = MetadataIndex()
app = {'name': 'App_Main', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['Core_Lib>=1,<3', 'Plugin[Fast]>=1; extra == "speed"']}
idx.add_candidate(app)
idx.add_candidate({'name': 'core-lib', 'version': '1.0'})
idx.add_candidate({'name': 'core-lib', 'version': '2.0'})
idx.add_candidate({'name': 'core-lib', 'version': '3.0'})
idx.add_candidate({'name': 'plugin', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['speed-lib>=1; extra == "fast"']})
idx.add_candidate({'name': 'speed-lib', 'version': '1.0'})
app['requires'].append('leak>=1')
res = idx.resolve(['App-Main[Speed]>=1'])
print(sorted(res['selected'].items()))
print(sorted((e['parent'], e['name'], tuple(e.get('extras') or ())) for e in res['edges']))
print(sorted((k, tuple(v)) for k, v in res['requested_extras'].items()))
print(res['dependents'].get('leak'), res['revision'] >= 6)

all_cases.description: A batch update and remove invalidates stale selection, exclusion, index, and reverse-dependent projections together.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import MetadataIndex
idx = MetadataIndex([
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['lib<4']},
    {'name': 'lib', 'version': '1.0'},
    {'name': 'lib', 'version': '2.0'},
    {'name': 'lib', 'version': '3.0'},
])
first = idx.resolve(['app>=1'])
idx.apply([
    {'action': 'update', 'candidate': {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['lib<3']}},
    {'action': 'remove', 'name': 'lib', 'version': '1.0'},
])
second = idx.resolve(['app>=1'])
print(sorted(first['selected'].items()), first['excluded']['lib'])
print(sorted(second['selected'].items()), second['excluded']['lib'])
print(idx.index()['lib'])
print(second['dependents']['lib'])

all_cases.description: dependents_of is a reverse projection of the same active graph used by resolve, including transitive parent lookup.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import MetadataIndex
idx = MetadataIndex([
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['core>=1']},
    {'name': 'core', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['shared>=1']},
    {'name': 'shared', 'version': '1.0'},
    {'name': 'plugin', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['shared>=1']},
])
res = idx.resolve(['app>=1'])
print(sorted((k, tuple(v)) for k, v in res['dependents'].items()))
print(idx.dependents_of('shared', roots=['app>=1']))
print(idx.dependents_of('shared', roots=['app>=1'], transitive=True))
print(idx.dependents_of('Shared', roots=['plugin>=1']))

all_cases.description: resolve_lock and apply_lock replay the same selected graph after unrelated candidate additions while fresh resolve can select a newer version.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import MetadataIndex
idx = MetadataIndex([
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['lib>=1']},
    {'name': 'lib', 'version': '1.0'},
    {'name': 'lib', 'version': '2.0'},
])
lock = idx.resolve_lock(['app>=1'])
idx.add_candidate({'name': 'lib', 'version': '3.0'})
idx.add_candidate({'name': 'other', 'version': '9.0'})
fresh = idx.resolve(['app>=1'])
replayed = idx.apply_lock(lock)
print(sorted(lock['selected'].items()))
print(sorted(fresh['selected'].items()))
print(sorted(replayed['selected'].items()))
print(replayed['lock_revision'] == lock['revision'], replayed['revision'] == idx.resolve(['app>=1'])['revision'])

])

all_cases.description: export_state/import_state replay the candidate table, resolver projections, reverse dependents, and lock behavior without relying on object identity.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import MetadataIndex
idx = MetadataIndex([
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['core>=1', 'docs[html]>=1; extra == "docs"']},
    {'name': 'core', 'version': '1.0'},
    {'name': 'docs', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['renderer>=1; extra == "html"']},
    {'name': 'renderer', 'version': '1.0'},
])
lock = idx.resolve_lock(['App[Docs]>=1'])
clone = MetadataIndex.import_state(idx.export_state())
a = idx.resolve(['App[Docs]>=1'])
b = clone.resolve(['App[Docs]>=1'])
replay = clone.apply_lock(lock)
print(clone.index() == idx.index())
print(a['selected'] == b['selected'], a['dependents'] == b['dependents'], a['requested_extras'] == b['requested_extras'])
print(sorted(replay['selected'].items()))
print(clone.dependents_of('renderer', roots=['app[docs]>=1'], transitive=True))

idx = MetadataIndex([
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['lib>=1']},
    {'name': 'lib', 'version': '1.0'},
])
before_state = idx.export_state()
before_res = idx.resolve(['app>=1'])
try:
    idx.apply([
        {'action': 'add', 'candidate': {'name': 'tool', 'version': '1.0'}},
        {'action': 'update', 'candidate': {'name': 'missing', 'version': '1.0'}},
    ])
    print('accepted')
except ValueError:
    print('batch-failed')
print(idx.export_state() == before_state, idx.resolve(['app>=1'])['selected'] == before_res['selected'])
idx.apply([
    {'action': 'add', 'candidate': {'name': 'tool', 'version': '1.0'}},
    {'action': 'update', 'candidate': {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['lib>=1', 'tool>=1']}},

])

b = idx.resolve(list(reversed(roots)))

all_cases.description: The stateless resolve_metadata convenience API and MetadataIndex.resolve expose equivalent semantic projections from the same candidate facts, while the index adds only lifecycle metadata.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import MetadataIndex, resolve_metadata
candidates = [
    {'name': 'app', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['addon[fast]>=1; extra == "speed"']},
    {'name': 'addon', 'version': '1.0', 'requires': ['fast-lib>=1; extra == "fast"']},
    {'name': 'fast-lib', 'version': '1.0'},
]
roots = ['App[Speed]>=1']
stateless = resolve_metadata(roots, candidates)
